compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of biological material. The P3 Pothos (anchor) and P2/P4 specimens are no longer visible as living entities.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: Pot is present but contains no visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No viable plant tissue detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos specimen has vanished/decomposed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No evidence of the specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of growth.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil is bare.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears desiccated and dark; no signs of moisture or fungal activity.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments remain in the black pot; paper receipt/debris present in later frames.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single leaf remnant in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive loss of the remaining leaf structure; signs of necrosis.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Total disappearance of all plant matter. The biome is now effectively a collection of empty pots and inert soil.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: Total mortality of all specimens. The environment is currently devoid of living botanical subjects.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue, absence of turgor pressure, and complete disappearance of previously observed leaf structures.

anomalies: Introduction of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment (failed to prevent collapse).
Introduction of paper receipt/debris in later frames.
vision_report.narrative_description: The sequence documents a rapid and total decline of the indoor botanical collection. Starting from a state of minimal viability (a single leaf), the specimens have undergone complete senescence or removal. The current state is a sterile environment consisting of empty pots and inert substrate. The white rabbit anchor remains, serving as a marker for a now-vacant habitat.
